firstAid.title: First Aid Kit
firstAid.subtitle: Quick relief exercises for difficult moments

grounding.title: 5-4-3-2-1 Grounding
grounding.intro: This technique helps you ground yourself when feeling anxious or overwhelmed by using your five senses.
grounding.instructions: You'll be guided through identifying things you can see, touch, hear, smell, and taste to bring your awareness to the present moment.
grounding.seePrompt: Look around and name 5 things you can see right now. Notice their colors, shapes, and details.
grounding.touchPrompt: Notice 4 things you can physically feel. The texture of your clothes, the chair beneath you, the temperature of the air.
grounding.hearPrompt: Listen carefully and identify 3 sounds around you. Maybe distant traffic, birds, or the hum of electronics.
grounding.smellPrompt: Notice 2 scents around you. If you can't smell anything, think of your favorite smells.
grounding.tastePrompt: Notice 1 thing you can taste, or recall your favorite taste. Perhaps take a sip of water.
grounding.complete: Great job! You've reconnected with the present moment

muscleRelaxation.title: Progressive Muscle Relaxation
muscleRelaxation.intro: This exercise helps you release physical tension by systematically tensing and relaxing different muscle groups.
muscleRelaxation.instructions: You'll tense each muscle group for 5 seconds, then release and relax for 10 seconds. Notice the difference between tension and relaxation.
muscleRelaxation.tip: Find a comfortable position and breathe naturally throughout the exercise.
muscleRelaxation.complete: Excellent! Your body should feel more relaxed now
muscleRelaxation.handsTense: Make tight fists with both hands. Squeeze as hard as you can.
muscleRelaxation.handsRelax: Release your fists completely. Let your fingers unfurl and notice the warmth flowing into your hands.
muscleRelaxation.armsTense: Bend your elbows and tense your biceps as hard as you can.
muscleRelaxation.armsRelax: Let your arms drop and hang loosely. Feel the tension melting away.
muscleRelaxation.shouldersTense: Raise your shoulders up toward your ears as high as possible.
muscleRelaxation.shouldersRelax: Drop your shoulders down. Feel them becoming heavy and relaxed.
muscleRelaxation.faceTense: Scrunch up your entire face - squeeze your eyes shut, wrinkle your nose, purse your lips.
muscleRelaxation.faceRelax: Let all the muscles in your face relax. Allow your jaw to drop slightly.
muscleRelaxation.stomachTense: Tighten your stomach muscles, pulling them in as if preparing for impact.
muscleRelaxation.stomachRelax: Release your stomach. Let it be soft and relaxed.
muscleRelaxation.legsTense: Tighten your thigh muscles by straightening your legs and tensing hard.
muscleRelaxation.legsRelax: Let your legs become completely limp and heavy.
muscleRelaxation.feetRelax: Release your toes. Let your feet rest comfortably.

affirmations.title: Positive Affirmations
affirmations.subtitle: Repeat these affirmations to yourself
affirmations.instruction: Take a deep breath and repeat this affirmation 3 times
affirmations.affirmation1: I am stronger than my challenges.
affirmations.affirmation3: I am growing and learning every day.
affirmations.affirmation4: I trust myself to handle what comes my way.
affirmations.affirmation5: I am worthy of love and respect.
affirmations.affirmation6: I choose to focus on what I can control.
affirmations.affirmation7: I am proud of how far I've come.
affirmations.affirmation8: I have the power to create positive change.
affirmations.affirmation10: I release what no longer serves me.
affirmations.affirmation11: I am exactly where I need to be.
affirmations.affirmation13: I am doing my best, and that is enough.
affirmations.affirmation14: I choose to be kind to myself.
affirmations.affirmation15: I am open to new possibilities.

worryJournal.title: Worry Journal
worryJournal.subtitle: Externalize your worries by writing them down
worryJournal.info: Writing down your worries can help you process them and reduce their power over you. This is a safe space to express what's on your mind.
worryJournal.placeholder: Write freely about what's bothering you...
worryJournal.savedMessage: Your worry has been recorded. Remember, acknowledging your worries is a step toward managing them.
worryJournal.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this entry?

distraction.title: Distraction Activities
distraction.subtitle: Choose an activity to help redirect your thoughts
distraction.info: These quick mental exercises can help shift your focus away from distressing thoughts.
distraction.countBackwardsInstruction:
Count backwards from 100 by 7s:
100, 93, 86, 79...

Take your time and focus on each number.
distraction.name5ThingsInstruction:
Name 5 things in different categories:

• 5 colors
• 5 animals
• 5 countries
• 5 foods
• 5 movies
distraction.alphabetGameInstruction:
Pick a category (animals, foods, etc.) and name one item for each letter of the alphabet:

A - Apple
B - Banana
C - Cherry...
distraction.colorHuntInstruction:
Look around your environment and find:

• 3 red things
• 3 blue things
• 3 green things
• 3 yellow things
distraction.wordAssociationInstruction:
Start with a word like 'happy' and create a chain where each word relates to the previous:

happy → sunshine → warm → summer → vacation...
distraction.bodyScanInstruction:
Notice each body part from head to toe:

• How does your head feel?
• Are your shoulders tense?
• How are your hands positioned?
• Notice your breathing
• Feel your feet on the ground
distraction.describeObjectInstruction:
Pick any object near you and describe it in detail:

• What color is it?
• What's its texture?
• How big is it?
• What's it made of?
• What's its purpose?

emergency.title: Emergency Contacts
emergency.description: Access helplines when you need support
emergency.subtitle: You don't have to face this alone
emergency.warning: If you're in immediate danger or experiencing a medical emergency, call 911 right away.
emergency.info: All of these services are free, confidential, and staffed by trained professionals who want to help.
emergency.textInstructions: To use this service, send a text message with "HELLO" to 741741 from your phone's messaging app.
emergency.suicidePreventionDesc: If you're thinking about suicide or need emotional support
emergency.crisisTextDesc: Text-based crisis support for any crisis situation
emergency.samhsaDesc: Mental health and substance abuse support
